raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By “make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) polishing wording only (clarity/tone without changing claims), (B) tightening the conceptual framing and internal consistency—including reconciling the ‘value-based’ claim with the described REINFORCE training and adjusting claims accordingly, or (C) emphasizing novelty/positioning versus prior work (comparisons/citations)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening primarily requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution claim so the value-based advantage and gap are explicit.",
    "R1.2": "The text labels QMaster as value-based while describing a policy-gradient/REINFORCE procedure, so internal argument consistency is critical.",
    "R1.3": "Claims such as “expert-level performance” and “superior sample efficiency” must align precisely with the reported evaluation setup and outcomes.",
    "R1.4": "Making the piece stronger entails articulating the delta versus nearest policy-gradient and value-based baselines to substantiate practical advantage.",
    "R1.6": "Concise, precise language and consistent terminology will materially improve perceived rigor and readability.",
    "RX.1": "Any strengthened claims should include clear uncertainty and limitations (e.g., variability, confidence, task coverage).",
    "RX.2": "Avoid overclaiming beyond the presented tasks and evidence, especially around ‘expert-level’ and general sample-efficiency benefits.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within strengthening the write-up rather than adding new experiments or unverifiable results.",
    "RX.4": "Prevent downstream misuse in robotics by avoiding misleading performance or safety implications.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear traceability from each claim to stated procedures, hyperparameters, tasks, and evaluation details."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By “make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) polishing wording only (clarity/tone without changing claims), (B) tightening the conceptual framing and internal consistency—including reconciling the ‘value-based’ claim with the described REINFORCE training and adjusting claims accordingly, or (C) emphasizing novelty/positioning versus prior work (comparisons/citations)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening primarily requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution claim so the value-based advantage and gap are explicit.
rationale.R1.2: The text labels QMaster as value-based while describing a policy-gradient/REINFORCE procedure, so internal argument consistency is critical.
rationale.R1.3: Claims such as “expert-level performance” and “superior sample efficiency” must align precisely with the reported evaluation setup and outcomes.
rationale.R1.4: Making the piece stronger entails articulating the delta versus nearest policy-gradient and value-based baselines to substantiate practical advantage.
rationale.R1.6: Concise, precise language and consistent terminology will materially improve perceived rigor and readability.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthened claims should include clear uncertainty and limitations (e.g., variability, confidence, task coverage).
rationale.RX.2: Avoid overclaiming beyond the presented tasks and evidence, especially around ‘expert-level’ and general sample-efficiency benefits.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within strengthening the write-up rather than adding new experiments or unverifiable results.
rationale.RX.4: Prevent downstream misuse in robotics by avoiding misleading performance or safety implications.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear traceability from each claim to stated procedures, hyperparameters, tasks, and evaluation details.
